title: Bug: Align Markdown link parsing between validation and index
description:
Project audit finding.

Context:
- `IndexService` resolves links to `target.md` and `target/index.md`, but `ValidationService` and `scripts/validate-pinake.mjs` only check the literal path.
- This creates false broken-link warnings for common Markdown links such as `[Guide](guide)` or `[Overview](folder/)`.
- Inline link extraction also does not handle optional Markdown link titles well, so `[Guide](guide.md "Read next")` can be treated as a bad target.

Action plan:
1. Add failing tests for extension validation and the standalone validator covering extensionless links, directory index links, anchors/query strings, and optional link titles.
2. Extract or duplicate a small shared resolver contract so validation and indexing agree on local link semantics.
3. Keep external links, mailto, tel, and pure anchor links ignored.
4. Update `scripts/validate-pinake.mjs` so CI output matches extension validation.
5. Add regression tests for `findBrokenReferences` so index behavior stays compatible.
6. Run compile, lint, standalone validator tests, and extension tests.

Acceptance checks:
- Extension validation and CI validation agree on the same local Markdown links.
- Extensionless and folder index links are not reported as broken when their targets exist.
- Optional link titles do not corrupt target parsing.

checklist.text: Add failing extension validation tests for extensionless, folder, anchor, query, and titled links
Add matching standalone validator tests or fixtures
Align ValidationService link resolution with IndexService semantics
Update scripts/validate-pinake.mjs to match extension behavior
Preserve ignored external, mailto, tel, and anchor-only link behavior
Run compile, lint, validator, and extension tests
